feed.title: Daily Digest
feed.subtitle: Quote, fact, life hack, fortune cookie, and headlines of the day, plus Polish public-holiday reminders

entry.title: The Uplift: The American Dream
entry.content: We celebrate the United States ahead of its 250th anniversary with stories about American heroes and pastimes. The founder of e.l.f. Cosmetics shares how he made a massive life change to become a Catholic priest.

entry.title: AI’s most important protocol is getting a little bit easier to use
entry.content: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is one of the basic building blocks of AI interoperability, giving AI models a secure way to access external data sources and services. It’s the plumbing that lets a chatbot reach into your calendar, your database, or your internal tools, instead of engineers building custom pipes for every connection. Next […]

entry.title: X relaunches a rebuilt Android app after year-long effort
entry.content: X says the rebuilt version of its Android app is now available globally.

entry.title: OpenAI is scared of open-weight models. Should the US be?
entry.content: Talk of banning Chinese-made open-weight LLMs reveals the challenge of turning AI into a business.

entry.title: Natural raises $30M to reinvent payments for AI agents — and take on Stripe
entry.content: The one-year-old startup aims to reinvent financial architecture for autonomous AI transactions.

entry.title: Top Seahawks storylines going into training camp 2026
entry.content: What is going to stand out the most for the Seahawks this summer?

entry.title: Rick Shiels gives his honest opinion on Bryson DeChambeau’s rules violation at The Open
entry.content: The Bryson DeChambeau fiasco at The Open Championship on Friday has certainly divided opinion. DeChambeau caused serious uproar at The Open last week when he was penalized two strokes for breaking the rules and subsequently threatened to withdraw from the championship.

entry.title: Judge pauses $110B Paramount-Warner Bros. merger
entry.content: The lawsuit from the states alleges that the deal would harm movie theaters, basic cable distributors, and audiences.

entry.title: Browns QB Shedeur Sanders opens up on relationship with Deshaun Watson
entry.content: While Shedeur Sanders and Deshaun Watson are deadlocked in a battle for the Browns' starting job, they're taking their relationship in stride

entry.title: Pat Riley Addresses Miami Heat’s Chances of Signing LeBron James
entry.content: Pat Riley and the Miami Heat want to sign LeBron James. After trading for Giannis Antetokounmpo, Riley said there is […]

entry.title: Judge says Paramount and Warner must halt merger for at least two weeks, granting states' request
entry.content: A federal judge on Monday ordered Paramount and Warner Bros. Discovery to halt their $81 billion merger for at least two weeks, allowing states that are challenging the deal more time to see their case through in court.

entry.title: Judge puts temporary pause on Paramount-Warner merger
entry.content: Paramount Skydance’s takeover of Warner Bros. Discovery hit a roadblock Monday after a federal judge temporarily paused the proposed merger.

entry.title: Ex-Olympian charged with damaging Reflecting Pool gets Sept. 28 trial date
entry.content: A judge in the nation’s capital has set a September trial date for a former Olympic canoe racer charged with deliberately damaging the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool

entry.title: The men behind Trump's $3.47 gas: An NFL coach, a GOP fundraiser and two New Jersey brothers
entry.content: President Donald Trump and his White House have enthusiastically promoted the Freedom Fuel Network, a chain of star-spangled convenience stores selling gas at $3.47 per gallon in honor of the 47th president.

entry.title: Political analysts see little chance of GOP renewing Planned Parenthood funding freeze
entry.content: Political strategists on both sides of the abortion debate say there's little chance of Congress renewing a Trump administration freeze on federal funding to Planned Parenthood that expired this month.

entry.title: Palantir isn’t the only option for the NHS | Letter
entry.content: Manchester’s integrated care board offers the health service a domestic alternative to the US firm, says Dr Devan MoodleyRegarding Peter Geoghegan’s article (I investigated Palantir’s foothold in the British state – and what I found should worry us all, 15 July), the nine years that Greater Manchester’s health system has spent without Palantir is a result not of caution but bold conviction.Greater Manchester’s integrated care board remains the only one in England to decline the Palantir-built federated data platform, having concluded that its own homegrown data capability was stronger. It built that capability with British universities, NHS clinicians and domestic technology firms. Continue reading...

entry.title: Trial begins for Massachusetts mom who says postpartum issues led her to kill 3 children
entry.content: Jury selection began Monday in the trial of a Massachusetts mother who strangled her three children while undergoing medical treatment for psychiatric problems that developed after she gave birth.

entry.title: Anti-ICE suspect arrested outside New York City federal building after lighting incendiary device
entry.content: Three people, including two firefighters, were injured Monday and a suspected anti-ICE activist hauling weapons was arrested after he lit an "incendiary device" outside a Manhattan building that houses immigration enforcement, the FBI and other federal agencies, federal officials said.

entry.title: Pamela Smart, serving life, seeks a new trial as a New Hampshire judge weighs her latest petition
entry.content: Lawyers for Pamela Smart, who is serving life in prison for orchestrating the murder of her husband by her teenage student in 1990, urged a New Hampshire judge Monday to allow her latest petition for a new trial to move forward.

entry.title: 3 women's world records set at the annual Lumberjack World Championships in Wisconsin
entry.content: Three new women's world records have been set at the Lumberjack World Championships competition held in a small Wisconsin community, full of high-powered sawing, chopping, logrolling, speed climbing and cheers of "Yo-Ho!"

entry.title: Ex-Mexican drug lord Ismael 'El Mayo' Zambada denounces violence as he's sentenced to life in prison
entry.content: Former Mexican drug kingpin Ismael "El Mayo" Zambada was sentenced in a U.S. court on Monday to life in prison and called for an end to the brand of brutal violence he used for decades to grow the Sinaloa cartel into the world's largest drug trafficking organization.

entry.content: Lawyers for Pamela Smart, who's serving life for orchestrating her husband's 1990 murder, are hoping that a New Hampshire judge will allow her latest petition for a new trial

entry.content: A shorebird known for its sharp, orange bill and beady eyes is making a comeback in North America thanks to a yearslong conservation effort

entry.title: Michigan athletics investigation finds 'weaknesses', changes promised
entry.content: The Jenner & Block law firm, which U-M brought in seven months back to investigate its athletic department culture, has completed its report.

entry.title: Lydia Ko talks to fellow Kiwi Ryan Fox after his British Open triumph
entry.content: Ko opened the call with, "Hello, Champion Golfer of the Year," pumping the air with her fist.

entry.title: Michigan AD Warde Manuel agrees to buyout, will step down at end of 2026
entry.content: The University of Michigan and embattled athletic director Warde Manuel have agreed to a buyout of the remaining years on his contract and he will step down at the end of the year, culminating a decade-long run with four national championships and a string of scandals. The school said Monday the decision was tied to investigations into former football coach Sherrone Moore's relationship with his executive assistant and a probe into the athletic department's culture. Manuel led a department behind this year's men's basketball title and a football program that won the 2023 national championship, but that wasn't enough for him to keep his job.

entry.title: Why Michigan AD Warde Manuel will step down at end of year after 'internal investigation'
entry.content: After changes atop both the football and basketball programs, Michigan will soon hire a new athletic director.

entry.title: A guide to 2026 WNBA All-Star weekend: Schedule, coaches, rosters

entry.content: Regional powers are worried about rising economic damage and the risk of escalation, but still face the challenge of getting the combatants on board.

entry.title: Why Spain cropped Donald Trump out of World Cup celebration photos
entry.content: While the U.S. president was initially on stage for Spain's celebrations, the team used a picture without Trump on social media.

entry.title: Gas prices rise above $4, on average, for the first time since mid-June
entry.content: The average U.S. price of gas rose above $4 per gallon on Monday as fighting in the oil-rich Middle East flares up.

entry.title: China's new AI model halts new subscriptions as demand swamps capacity
entry.content: The Chinese AI model Kimi K3 has suspended new subscriptions after overwhelming demand pushed its capacity close to its limits

entry.title: Lakers urged to sign $45M disruptive 3-and-D veteran regardless of Jonathan Kuminga's decision
entry.content: The Lakers are going after Jonathan Kuminga. However, they have been called on to sign a veteran free agent that would be massive help.

entry.title: He Bought a House ‘Sight Unseen.’ Then He Found Three Dead Bodies.
entry.content: Skeletal remains were discovered inside a Connecticut home sold through a foreclosure auction—an extreme example of what buyers can encounter when purchasing homes this way.

entry.title: Bold prediction: Giants GM Tony Vitello finds MLB trade deadline success, unloads high-priced veterans
entry.content: Entering a rebuilding phase, San Francisco's front office will attempt to move on from a few multi-year veteran deals.

entry.title: Ewan Mitchell helps Aemond show his true colors in 'House of the Dragon'
entry.content: The British actor, who plays Aemond Targaryen in HBO's "House of the Dragon," spoke in depth about Season 3, Episode 5, which examines his relationship with Alys Rivers.

entry.content: The Indiana Fever fan favorite is working hard.

entry.title: Retired since 2015, Michael Vick still earns a six-figure payday from the NFL
entry.content: More than a decade after his final NFL game, Michael Vick is still collecting a six-figure annual payment from the league. The former Falcons star reveals the retirement benefits and financial decisions that have provided him with lifetime security—and why he says too many former players miss out.

entry.title: FBI shifts immigration agent cases to HSI after recent fatal shootings, sources say
entry.content: HSI is an arm of ICE, meaning the immigration enforcement agency would effectively be investigating incidents involving its own agents.

entry.title: This Date in MMA History: When Conor McGregor still was beloved at UFC Dublin
entry.content: It's been a dozen years since Conor McGregor officially hit superstar status when he put away Diego Brandao in front of his home fans in Ireland.

entry.title: Third time's a charm for golfer Ryan Sam at Stark County Amateur
entry.content: After coming close the previous two seasons, a former Youngstown State golfer has won his first Stark Amateur.

entry.title: What we learned from Stefanos Tsitsipas, Andrey Rublev ATP tournament wins
entry.content: Stefanos Tsitsipas and Andrey Rublev, veteran ATP tennis players, won their respective tournaments in Gstaad and Bastad, respectively. For Tsitsipas, the Swiss Open Gstaad ATP 250 is his first title in over a year. It is his 13th overall, and…

entry.title: Scaloni leaves news conference in tears talking about his future
entry.content: Argentina manager Lionel Scaloni leaves his post-match news conference after Sunday's World Cup final defeat by Spain in tears.

entry.title: Straight Talk: Yamamoto in complete control, goes distance for first time
entry.content: "I was able to throw without any wasted effort and my location was good, so a lot of at-bats ended quickly and I think that led to me having a good day on the mound."

entry.title: Why was Enzo Fernandez sent off in World Cup final?
entry.content: Fernandez’s second yellow card offence was criticised by Alan Shearer and called ‘stupid’ after Argentina were left down to 10 men

entry.title: Watch Lionel Messi become emotional after loss to Spain in what could be legend's last World Cup game
entry.content: Lionel Messi's eyes grew wet and wild following the end of Argentina's loss to Spain in the World Cup final.

entry.title: Recess Is Disappearing. Parents Are Pushing to Bring It Back.

entry.title: Fortune Cookie of the Day
entry.content:
The best time is NOW.
Lucky Numbers: 12, 25, 34, 88, 98, 30

entry.title: Life Hack of the Day
entry.content: Don't take advice from people who has not done the thing you want to do.

entry.title: Useless Fact of the Day
entry.content: Male bats have the highest rate of homosexuality of any mammal.

entry.title: Quote of the Day — Genghis Khan
entry.content: “An action committed in anger is an action doomed to failure.” — Genghis Khan

entry.title: Second Lady Usha Vance Gives Birth to Baby Boy
entry.content: The vice president isn’t expected to take formal paternity leave, but will plan his schedule around time with new son.

entry.title: Rodri wins Golden Ball as Spain reigns at 2026 World Cup
entry.content: Spain's Rodri was awarded the Golden Ball after Spain's victory over Argentina in the 2026 World Cup final.

entry.title: New Zealand beats West Indies in a 1-wicket thriller to win ODI series
entry.content: Mark Chapman made 80 and Mitchell Santner shepherded the tail to guide New Zealand to a tense one-wicket win over West Indies in the fourth one-day international Sunday and to an unassailable 3-1 lead in the five-match series. Santner was dropped with three runs needed and one wicket in hand, and last man Jayden Lennox survived reviews for caught behind and lbw, before New Zealand edged past West Indies' modest total of 188 with 35 balls remaining. The New Zealand captain hit the winning runs from the first ball of the 45th over to finish 34 not out and to spoil West Indies' hopes of achieving a series-leveling victory to pay tribute to cricket great Garry Sobers.

entry.title: Watch: Spain fans erupt in celebration after World Cup final victory
entry.content: Fans in Madrid and Barcelona celebrated long into the night after Spain defeated Argentina 1-0 in extra time to win their second FIFA World Cup title.

entry.title: Kentucky adds a late JUCO running back for the 2026 season
entry.content: The Kentucky Wildcats and Will Stein have gained a commitment for the 2026 season from a junior college running back.

entry.title: Messi denied dream farewell as abject Argentina lose World Cup final
entry.content: Lionel Messi could have ended all debate over who is the greatest player of all time by winning another World Cup with Argentina on Sunday, but he was left in tears following Spain's 1-0 win.The game ended 0-0, before Messi missed his penalty in the shootout as Argentina lost again.

entry.title: U.S. Servicemember Killed in Iraq as Troop Death Toll in Jordan Likely to Rise
entry.content: Separately, work is under way to identify human remains at a base in Jordan after a missile struck the location this weekend, U.S. Central Command said.

entry.title: American service member killed in Iraq as U.S. strikes against Iran enter 2nd week
entry.content: Two U.S. service members were killed in Jordan, and a third is missing in action, U.S. Central Command said.

entry.title: Dozens missing after passenger ferry sinks off coast of Guyana
entry.content: Emergency response has rescued 67 people, including 15 children, from one of the country’s worst maritime disastersA ferry carrying 116 passengers and crew sank ⁠off the coast of Guyana in South America ⁠late Saturday, with 67 ⁠people, ​including 15 children, rescued so far, according to Guyana ⁠authorities.A massive search-and-rescue operation continues for dozens of missing passengers in what has been described by officials as one of the country’s worst maritime disasters. Continue reading...

entry.title: How Fox kept cool at baked Birkdale to win The Open
entry.content: Ryan Fox wins the Open Championship - his first major title - with his final putt on a record-breaking weekend at Royal Birkdale.

entry.title: Fleetwood won't give up Open dream after 'special' week
entry.content: Tommy Fleetwood says he will continue to chase his dream of winning The Open after falling short at the end of a "special week" on home soil at Royal Birkdale.

entry.title: Official: Málaga announce new signing from Leganés
entry.content: La Liga newcomers Málaga have late this week announced a fresh addition to the club’s first-team ranks. This comes by way of confirmation that Juan Cruz has been brought in for the duration of the...
